content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Charles J. McKee 
Monterey County Counsel 
168 West Alisa) Street 3rd Floor 
Salinas, CA 93901 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-155 
Dear Mr. McKee: 
July I 9, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Monterey's Chief Assistant 
County Counsel Leslie Girard regarding his responsibilities under the conflict of interest provisions 
of the Political Refonn Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that: ( 1) we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section 1090; the Commission is not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re 
Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71), any advice we provide assumes your facts are accurate and 
complete, and (2) the Commission does not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 
18329(b)(8)(A).) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that 
may have already taken place, and any conclusions contained in this letter apply only to prospective 
actions. 
QUESTIONS 
In your July 13, 2017 correspondence, you restated you questions as follows: 
1. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the Salinas Valley Basin 
Groundwater Sustainability Agency (the "JPA'') regarding the imposition of fees and charges on all 
persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin? 
2. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA regarding the imposition of 
fees and charges on persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater 
Basin that are similarly situated to California American Water Company ("California American") 
(i.e., domestic/urban extractors)? 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


No. A-17-155 
Page No. 2 
3. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA regarding the imposition of 
fees and charges on persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater 
Basin that are not similarly situated to California American (i.e. agricultural extractors)? 
4. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JP A on the imposition of 
regulatory requirements, including pumping limits, on all persons or entities pumping groundwater 
from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin? 
5. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA on the imposition of 
regulatory requirements, including pumping limits, on persons or entities pumping groundwater 
from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin that are similarly situated to California American (i.e., 
domestic/urban extractors)? 
6. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA on the imposition of 
regulatory requirements, including pumping limits, on persons or entities pumping groundwater 
from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin that are not similarly John Wallace, Assistant General 
Counsel situated to California American (i.e., agricultural extractors)? 
7. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA on any matters not 
involving the subjects of above (for example general government law, Brown Act, Public Contracts 
Code, etc.)? 
8. If any of the above circumstances present a prohibited conflict of interest for Mr. Girard, 
provided he recuses himself from participation in any decision regarding such matters, and provided 
no other attorney in the Monterey County Counsel's Office has any financial interest in California 
American, does the Office of the Monterey County Counsel have a prohibited conflict of interest in 
advising on such matters (for example through the services of a different attorney)? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1-6. Since California American does a very small percentage of its pumping in the JPA 's 
jurisdiction, it does not appear that any of the decisions you specified will contribute to a change in 
the value of a California American. Thus, Mr. Girard will not have a conflict of interest. 
7. The Act only regulates financial conflicts of interests; thus, Mr. Girard may fully 
participate in any decisions of the JPA that do not affect his financial interests (including his 
interest in California American), including general government matters that do not impact 
California American including the Brown Act questiops, conflict of interest issues, personnel 
actions, contracts, etc., not related to matters affecting California American. 
8. We have concluded that no conflict of interest exists under the facts provided. 


FACTS 
No. A-17-155 
Page No. 3 
The JPA: Monterey County Counsel is statutorily designated counsel to the County of 
Monterey ("County") and the Monterey County Water Resources Agency (the "Water Agency"). In 
December of 2016, the Monterey County Board of Supervisors and the Board of Supervisors for the 
Water Agency approved joining the JPA to sustainably manage groundwater within the Basin. 
• The JPA has the authority to levy fees and assessments within its boundaries, and impose 
regulatory requirements for the purpose of sustainably managing the basin's groundwater, 
pursuant to the California Sustainable Groundwater Management Act ("SGMA").2 
• The boundaries of the JP A are those of the Basin within Monterey County but do not 
include the adjudicated Seaside sub-basis, or the jurisdictional boundaries of the Marina 
Coast Water District or the City of Greenfield. 
• 
Pursuant to the JPA agreement, the GSA currently receives legal services from the County, 
through County Counsel, at no cost through September 30, 2017. The County also provided 
start-up funds to the JP A. 
• The JPA issued a Request for Qualifications ("RFQ") for legal services to be provided 
beyond September 30, 2017, and the County is considering submitting a proposal, which has 
prompted this request for formal advice. 
• The JP A agreement also provides that JP A members will contribute certain sums to fund the 
activities of the JPA through its first two fiscal years (through June 30, 2019). It is not 
anticipated that the JPA will consider levying any fees or assessments until after that time. 
Mr. Girard 's Interests: Mr. Girard is the Chief Assistant County Counsel for Monterey 
County. California American is an investor owned utility regulated by the Public Utilities 
Commission and a subsidiary of American Water Company (aka American Water Works Company 
Inc.), a publicly traded company on the New York Stock Exchange.3 
Mr. Girard's spouse is employed by California American. Ms. Girard's income is not 
dependent on California American's volume of water sales in Monterey County. You also stated 
that Mr. Girard has not and will not participate in any matter where California American is a 
claimant, applicant, respondent, contracting party, or is otherwise named or identified as the subject 
of the proceeding. 
2 Further you stated that where the JPA did consider and decide regulatory matters or financing mechanisms 
that may cause a fee or assessment to be levied or the regulations imposed, there would be a number of subsequent 
intervening events. not including JPA decisions, that would need to take place before there was any actual 
implementation of the decisions. 
l On July 13, 2017, you clarified that California American does not have direct investors; it is a wholly owned 
subsidiary of American Water Company, which is traded on the New York Stock Exchange (symbol: A WK) which has 
direct investors. California American is doing business in California, is considered an investor owned utility regulated 
by the PUC. 


No. A-17-155 
Page No. 4 
The Basin consists of approximately 539,140 acres. The Water Agency, which has had 
water management responsibility within the Basin until the adoption of SGMA, has records for 
approximately 11,400 wells in the Basin. There are other wells in existence that are currently not 
"known" because, for example, they are old wells or no record was ever submitted for the well. Of 
the 11,400 wells that are known, approximately 1,380 are designated as domestic/municipal wells 
and 2,500 are designated as agricultural wells. The use (i.e. domestic/municipal/ag/other) of the 
majority of the remaining known wells is unknown. There are five PUC regulated water companies 
in the Basin, California Water Service Company ("Cal Water"), Alisa! Water Company ("ALCO"), 
Cal Am, Little Bear Water Company, and Spreckels Water Company. There are also many small 
private water systems, mutual water companies and water districts supplying domestic users. In 
addition, many large fanns in the Valley pump irrigation water from high capacity wells. With 
respect to California American: 
• 
According to data on file with the California Department of Water Resources the majority of 
California American's service territory is outside the Basin. 
• 
California American operates a few small domestic water systems whose groundwater 
supply is in the Basin, where the JPA has jurisdiction. These are identified as the Ambler, 
Chualar, Ralph Lane, and Toro systems. You clarified in your July 14, 2017 correspondence 
that: 
o California American's service areas in the Basin is estimated to represent 8.5% of 
California American's entire service territory in Monterey County (3,500/40,950). 
o Also, as you stated in your original letter, California America's service areas within 
the Basin represent only 0.65% of the total Basin area (3,500/539,140). 
o Finally, according to the Urban Water Management Plans for all of California 
American's service territories in California (Monterey, Sacramento, Los Angeles, 
Ventura and San Diego) you stated that California American's total service territory 
consists of 124,378 acres. The service area within the jurisdiction of the JPA thus 
equals less than 2.8% of California American's entire service territory 
(3,500/124,378). 
• 
In 2015 California American pumped a total of 446 acre feet from the Basin to serve these 
areas. A total of 514,714 acre feet of water was pumped from the Basin in 2015. By 
contrast, you stated that Cal Water's Salinas District, whose territory is entirely within the 
Basin, pumped 14,658 acre feet of water from the Basin in 2015. Thus, in 2015 California 
American pumped just 0.09% of total extractions from the Basin, and approximately 3% of 
Cal Water's extractions. California American projects to pump 500 acre feet per year 
through 2035. You clarified in your July 14, 2017 correspondence that: 
o You estimate that in 2015 California American pumped a total of 10,217 acre feet of 
water from all its sources in Monterey County. Thus, in 2015 California American 
pumped 9,771 acre feet of water outside of the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin 
( I 0,217 - 446), and California American's pumping of 446 acre feet in the Basin 
represented approximately 4.4% of its total pumping from all sources in Monterey 
County in that year ( 446/ 10,217). 
o In 2020 California American projects to pump 500 acre feet from the Basin, with a 
total pumping of 10,835 acre feet from all sources in Monterey County. Thus in 


No. A-17-155 
Page No. 5 
2020 California American's pumping from the Basin will represent 4.6% of its total 
pumping from all sources in Monterey County in that year (500/10,835). 
· o On a statewide basis, California American's pumping in the Basin represents an 
extremely small percentage of overall groundwater extractions. Based on the Urban 
Water Management Plans for all of California American's service territories 
California American pumped a total of 53,477 acre feet of water in 2015 (the San 
Diego and Ventura District do not pump groundwater). The extraction of 446 acre 
feet from the Basin thus represents 0.8% of total groundwater pumping by California 
American statewide (446/53,477). According to the same filings, future pumping in 
2020 will continue to represent only 0.8% of all groundwater pumping statewide 
( 500/61493 ). 
• In addition, California American has proposed to build a desalination plant on the coast of 
Monterey Bay within the County, the Basin and JPA territory. The desalination plant is 
proposed to be built within the northern coastal boundaries of the City of Marina. Currently, 
California American has drilled and is operating "test wells" to determine whether there is 
any impact on the waters of the Basin from their operation, or whether there is no such 
impact and the wells instead draw on brackish sea water. In your July 13, 2017, 
correspondence you also noted that California American provides some wastewater 
treatment service in the Basin but you believe that service to be minimal. California 
American provides no other service in the jurisdiction of the JP A. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
(Section 87103.) A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable financial 
effect of a governmental decision on a public official's interests is material. Different standards 
apply to determine whether a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on an interest will be material 
depending on the nature of the interest. 
Interests from which a conflict of interest may arise are defined in Section 87103. The 
interest implicated by your facts is an interest in a source of income to the official which aggregates 
to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c).) "Income" includes any 
community property interest in the income of a spouse. (Section 82030(a).) 
However, in order for any financial interest to be the basis for a conflict of interest, the 
specific decision in q1,1estion must have a foreseeable and material financial effect on the interest. 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
As an initial matter, since the Act only regulates financial conflicts of interests, Mr. Girard 
may fully participate in any decisions of the JP A that do not affect his financial interests, such as 
general government matters that do not impact California American including the Brown Act, 
conflict of interest issues, personnel actions, contracts, etc., not related to matters affecting 
California American. 


No. A-17-155 
Page No. 6 
In all other cases, the following standards apply depending on the specific facts of a 
decision. 
(I) Regulation 1870l(a) provides that a financial effect is presumed to be reasonably 
foreseeable if the financial interest is a named party in, or the subject of, a governmental decision 
before the official or the official's agency. A financial interest is the subject of a proceeding if the 
decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or 
other entitlement to, or contract with, the financial interest. 
You stated that Mr. Girard will not participate in any matter where California American is a 
claimant, applicant, respondent, contracting party, or is otherwise named or identified as the subject 
of the proceeding, Thus, the decisions you ask about would not explicitly involve California 
American.4 
(2) Where the financial interest is not explicitly involved in the decision, a financial effect is 
reasonably foreseeable where it "can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than 
hypothetical or theoretical ... absent extraordinary circumstances beyond the public official's 
control." (Regulation 1870l(b).) A financial effect need not be likely to be considered reasonably 
foreseeable. (Ibid.) 
With respect to materiality, Regulation 18702.3 provides that the effect is material if: "[t]he 
source is a business entity that will be financially affected under the standards as applied to a 
financial interest in Regulation 18702.1." Regulation I 8702. l(b) provides that a decision's effect on 
a privately-held business entity is material if a prudent person with sufficient information would 
find it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision's financial effect would contribute to a change in 
the price of the business entity's publicly traded stock, or the value of a privately-held business 
entity. 
You estimate that in 2015 California American pumped a total of I 0,217 acre feet of water 
from all its sources in Monterey County, 9,771 acre feet of water outside of the Salinas Valley 
Groundwater Basin. This is approximately 4.4% of California American's total pumping from all 
sources in Monterey County in that year. (446/10,217). Moreover, this is just 0.8% of the 
groundwater pumped by California America statewide. (446/53,477). Based on this data, it appears 
unlikely that the JPA imposition of fees and charges on persons or entities pumping groundwater 
from the from the jurisdiction (including California American) would contribute to a change in the 
value of a California American. Similarly, the imposition of regulatory requirements, including 
pumping limits, on all persons or entities pumping groundwater, would not contribute to a change in 
the value o.f a California American. 
4 You also stated that California American has proposed to build a desalination plant on the coast of Monterey 
Bay within the County, the Basin and JPA territory. We assume that Mr. Girard will also disqualify from any JPA or 
county decisions concerning the plant, were California American to come before those agencies regarding the plant. 


No. A-17-155 
Page No. 7 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Jack Woodside 
JWW:jgl

8. We have concluded that no conflict of interest exists under the facts provided.

QUESTION: In your July 13, 2017 correspondence, you restated you questions as follows: 
1. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the Salinas Valley Basin 
Groundwater Sustainability Agency (the "JPA'') regarding the imposition of fees and charges on all 
persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin? 
2. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA regarding the imposition of 
fees and charges on persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater 
Basin that are similarly situated to California American Water Company ("California American") 
(i.e., domestic/urban extractors)? 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained inNo. A-17-155  
3. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA regarding the imposition of 
fees and charges on persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater 
Basin that are not similarly situated to California American (i.e. agricultural extractors)? 
4. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JP A on the imposition of 
regulatory requirements, including pumping limits, on all persons or entities pumping groundwater 
from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin? 
5. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA on the imposition of 
regulatory requirements, including pumping limits, on persons or entities pumping groundwater 
from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin that are similarly situated to California American (i.e., 
domestic/urban extractors)? 
6. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA on the imposition of 
regulatory requirements, including pumping limits, on persons or entities pumping groundwater 
from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin that are not similarly John Wallace, Assistant General 
Counsel situated to California American (i.e., agricultural extractors)? 
7. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA on any matters not 
involving the subjects of above (for example general government law, Brown Act, Public Contracts 
Code, etc.)? 
8. If any of the above circumstances present a prohibited conflict of interest for Mr. Girard, 
provided he recuses himself from participation in any decision regarding such matters, and provided 
no other attorney in the Monterey County Counsel's Office has any financial interest in California 
American, does the Office of the Monterey County Counsel have a prohibited conflict of interest in 
advising on such matters (for example through the services of a different attorney)?

CONCLUSION: 1-6. Since California American does a very small percentage of its pumping in the JPA 's 
jurisdiction, it does not appear that any of the decisions you specified will contribute to a change in 
the value of a California American. Thus, Mr. Girard will not have a conflict of interest. 
7. The Act only regulates financial conflicts of interests; thus, Mr. Girard may fully 
participate in any decisions of the JPA that do not affect his financial interests (including his 
interest in California American), including general government matters that do not impact 
California American including the Brown Act questiops, conflict of interest issues, personnel 
actions, contracts, etc., not related to matters affecting California American. 
8. We have concluded that no conflict of interest exists under the facts provided.

McKee Monterey County Counsel 168 West Alisa) Street 3rd Floor Salinas, CA 93901 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-17-155 Dear Mr. McKee: July I 9, 2017 This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Monterey's Chief Assistant County Counsel Leslie Girard regarding his responsibilities under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Refonn Act (the "Act").1 Please note that: ( 1) we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090; the Commission is not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71), any advice we provide assumes your facts are accurate and complete, and (2) the Commission does not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that may have already taken place, and any conclusions contained in this letter apply only to prospective actions. QUESTIONS In your July 13, 2017 correspondence, you restated you questions as follows: 1. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the Salinas Valley Basin Groundwater Sustainability Agency (the "JPA'') regarding the imposition of fees and charges on all persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin? 2. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA regarding the imposition of fees and charges on persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin that are similarly situated to California American Water Company ("California American") (i.e., domestic/urban extractors)? 1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. No. A-17-155 Page No. 2 3. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA regarding the imposition of fees and charges on persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin that are not similarly situated to California American (i.e. agricultural extractors)? 4. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JP A on the imposition of regulatory requirements, including pumping limits, on all persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin? 5. Will Mr. Girard have a conflict of interest in advising the JPA on the imposition of regulatory requirements, including pumping limits, on persons or entities pumping groundwater from the Salinas Valley Groundwater Basin that are similarly situated to California American (i.e., domestic/urban extractors)? 6.
